title: Stock Market English Explained in 15 minutes 🚀
description:
Want to understand English for investors? 
In this video, I break down a real stock page on Yahoo Finance and explain the most important investing terms in simple, clear English. 🇬🇧🇺🇸

You’ll learn what a ticker is, how to understand market cap, what the P/E ratio really tells you — and how to talk about all of it like a pro.

Dołącz do społeczności, wspieraj rozwój kanału, i odblokuj więcej treści  ➡️ https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpaTGe1ISA6kf3e6feVtKog/join

00:00 Intro
00:47 Yahoo Finance
01:13 Stock Page
01:28 Ticker
02:10 The Price
03:48 Market Closed
04:23 Change Values
05:34 Summary Box
05:46 Previous Close
06:05 Open
06:28 Day Range 
06:54 Week Range 
07:15 Market Cap
07:35 Beta 
10:00 P/E ratio
10:56 Dividend Yield
12:09 Ex-Divdend Date 
12:21 Discussing the chart
14:57 Outro

But due to the fact that the market is closed, we have the pre-market price right here. Remember markets open and then markets close. Now given that these values are in green, we can see the numbers are in green. That means there is a positive change. the value has gone up. If the value had gone down, these numbers, these percentages would be in red, as we can actually see on the pre-market figure. So, we've got two change values here. A raw price change, meaning how many dollars has it gone up by or gone down by? And in this case, it's gone up by $2.96, right? And then we've got the percentage price change. So in Nvidia's case, we actually have a massive range from $86 all the way up to $174. So now we're looking at the market cap capitalizing, the market capitalization, which is simply the total value of all outstanding shares of Nvidia. And it's massive. It is 4.237 trillion. The next key metric we're looking at is beta. Beta is essentially a volatility metric that compares the volatility of any given stock to the way the entire market is moving. So volatility is how volatile is the stock? How volatile is the price? Is it very volatile? In general, we also assess markets by volatility, too. For example, the cryptocurrency market is very volatile. Prices going up and down all the time. It's not very stable. A beta of one essentially is a kind of standard in line with the market.
